publicationStmt.p: Transcribed from Bodleian Library, Lovelace Byron collection, Box 166, folios 72-73. Transcription by Lisa Gold (2022). TEI conversion for Sextant pipeline, 2026.

div.p: Dearest W. I am vexed I did not give you the drawing-book, when you went. I am sure you would have made me a sketch or two.
I am longing to read you some some most curious & interesting things about ferns, & also about the preservation of plants in glass cases. I am quite pleased
to have discovered such a vein of amusement, & which I think we may also turn to no small practical account.

I had a very long ride yesterday, & was enchanted with the beauty of everything, & with the splendour of a view which I had never seen before. I rode all along the Downs (just this side of St Martha's) to the Telegraph near Guildford, when the whole Town & valley burst upon me like

a fairy-scene; then I went down by the old castle, & turned to Shalford, whence I came back thro' all those wild & picturesque dingles & jungles & woods &c, that run parallel with the Downs I had been shortly before gallopping along.--
I was more at ease too, & courageous, than I have been for some time.--
Charlotte is in bed with another boy, & everything doing extremely well.-- I

think you have better send on Charles Murray's letter to my mother. I cannot conceive what could make him so desperately in a hurry to dispatch her statement, without a day's delay even. He seems to think the matter by no means pressing; & we have (amongst us all) had to add to the complexity of the most complex piece of business the world ever saw; whereas a little delay would have made it simple & consistent enough.
What a treat to get a letter from the Hen without any allusions

even to E. L N. B V. B }; [the three pairs of initials are linked together with a large single brace, followed directly to the right by a semicolon and the following text] Three combinations of letters which I regard with particular horror & spite. [curved line]
